headerDisplay: Exposes one HomeKit contact sensor per facade. A sensor is open while the sun illuminates that facade (azimuth range plus a minimum altitude). Wire the sensors to scenes in the Home app yourself.

latitude.title: Latitude
latitude.description: Geographic latitude in decimal degrees (north–south axis). Range −90 to 90; positive = north, negative = south. In Google Maps and most apps a place's coordinates are shown as 'latitude, longitude' — copy the FIRST number here. Example: 52.37.

longitude.title: Longitude
longitude.description: Geographic longitude in decimal degrees (east–west axis). Range −180 to 180; positive = east, negative = west. Coordinates are usually shown as 'latitude, longitude' — copy the SECOND number here. Example: 9.74.

name.title: Name
name.description: Sensor name shown in the Home app. Must not be empty.

azimuthMin.title: Azimuth min (deg, 0=N 90=E 180=S 270=W)
azimuthMin.description: Start of the sky sector the sun must be in (compass degrees). If min > max the range wraps across 0°/360° (e.g. 315→45 for north).

azimuthMax.title: Azimuth max (deg, 0=N 90=E 180=S 270=W)
azimuthMax.description: End of the sky sector the sun must be in (compass degrees).

altitudeMin.title: Minimum altitude (deg above horizon)
altitudeMin.description: The sensor only opens when the sun is at least this high above the horizon. 0 = horizon; raise it to ignore low sun behind neighbouring houses.
